subtitles.original: ミルフィーユ ワッフル ポンポン
シュークリーム バナナゼリー
subtitles.english: Chou à la crème, banana jelly

subtitles.original: ホットケーキ バームクーヘン
バター 渡狸クレープ
ワタヌキと北京ダック
ハチミツみたいにとろける
柿食うっけ？ こんなにも
subtitles.english: Ka, Ki, Ku, Ke, are you eating persimmons? Like this too

subtitles.original: さしすせ ソフトクリーム
subtitles.english: Sa, Shi, Su, Se, soft-serve ice cream

subtitles.original: たちつて トルテ来た
subtitles.english: Ta, Chi, Tsu, Te, torte has come
Na, Ni, Nu, Ne, salted seaweed

subtitles.original: はっ！ヒフヘ ホットパイと
subtitles.english: Ha! Hi, Fu, He, with hot pie
The two of us, nice and warm

subtitles.original: まみむめ モンブラン
やいゆえ ヨーグルト
ラリるれ ロールケーキ
subtitles.english: Ra, Ri, Ru, Re, roll cake

subtitles.original: 好き好きラブ・コンボ
ようこそジャパリパークへ
Welcome to ようこそジャパリパーク！
subtitles.english: Welcome to, welcome to Japari Park!

subtitles.original: 今日もドッタンバッタン大騒ぎ
subtitles.english: Today, too, it's a great uproar, tumbling and falling

subtitles.original: 高らかに笑い笑えば フレンズ
subtitles.english: If you laugh out loud, you're a friend

subtitles.original: 喧嘩して すっちゃかめっちゃかしても 仲良し
subtitles.english: Even if we fight and make a mess, we're best friends

subtitles.original: けものは居ても のけものは居ない
subtitles.english: Even if there are animals, there are no outcasts

subtitles.original: 本当の愛はここにある
ほら 君も手をつないで大冒険
subtitles.english: Come on, hold hands and go on a great adventure

subtitles.original: Welcome to ようこそジャパリパーク！
subtitles.english: Welcome to, welcome to Japari Park!

subtitles.original: 今日もドッタンバッタン大騒ぎ
subtitles.english: Today, too, it's a great uproar, tumbling and falling
Everyone has different appearances and shapes
That's why we are attracted to each other

subtitles.original: 夕暮れ空に 指をそっと重ねたら
subtitles.english: If we rest our fingers gently against the twilight sky

subtitles.original: 君をもっと知りたいな
subtitles.english: I want to know you more

subtitles.original: 振り向けば あちらこちらで トラブル
subtitles.english: If you look back, there's trouble here and there
I don't understand any of it

subtitles.original: けものですもの 大目に見てね
subtitles.english: We're animals, so please overlook it

subtitles.original: みんな自由に生きている
そう 君も飾らなくたって大丈夫
subtitles.english: Yes, you don't have to put on an act either, it's okay

subtitles.original: Nice to meet you \u200eジャパリパーク！
subtitles.english: Nice to meet you, Japari Park!

subtitles.original: 今日からはどうぞよろしくね
subtitles.english: From today, I'm counting on you

subtitles.original: いつもいつでも優しい笑顔
subtitles.english: Always, forever with a kind smile

subtitles.original: 開かれた扉 夢をいっぱい語ったら
subtitles.english: If we talk a lot about our dreams after the gate is open

subtitles.original: どこまででも どこまででも
subtitles.english: No matter how far, no matter how far

subtitles.original: 続いていく グレートジャーニー
subtitles.english: It will continue, a great journey

subtitles.original: 世界中に響け サファリメロディ
subtitles.english: Let the safari melody echo throughout the world

subtitles.original: Welcome to ようこそジャパリパーク！
subtitles.english: Welcome to, welcome to Japari Park!

subtitles.original: Welcome to ようこそジャパリパーク！
subtitles.english: Welcome to, welcome to Japari Park!

subtitles.original: 今日もドッタンバッタン大騒ぎ
subtitles.english: Today, too, it's a great uproar, tumbling and falling
Everyone has different appearances and shapes
That's why we are attracted to each other

subtitles.original: 夕暮れ空に 指をそっと重ねたら
subtitles.english: If we rest our fingers gently against the twilight sky

subtitles.original: 君をもっと知りたいな
subtitles.english: I want to know you more

subtitles.original: Oh, Welcome to the ジャパリパーク！
subtitles.english: Oh, Welcome to the Japari Park!

subtitles.original: Oh, Welcome to the ジャパリパーク！
subtitles.english: Oh, Welcome to the Japari Park!

responses.thoughts:
**Final Subtitle Generation Plan**

Alright, as an expert in subtitle synchronization, here's my plan to tackle this task. My primary goal is to produce audio-synchronized subtitles in JSON format, adhering strictly to the given constraints. The output must be delivered through the `default_api:final_result` call.

The core of this process involves a deep dive into the provided text and audio, guided by a set of well-defined rules. The **Vocal Events Rule** is paramount: only subtitle when there's vocal activation. Aggressive truncation is key to avoid overlapping subtitles. The **Visual Events Rule** dictates that on-screen text, especially lyrics, takes precedence, even when there's no corresponding audio, but must be displayed if visible.

I'll be using a **Fallback Hierarchy**: firstly, hardcoded subtitles or lyrics (if any), then scene context, then finally the provided reference JSON, which I'll treat purely as a spelling guide, *not* as a script to be copied blindly. **Grounding Instructions** are critical – any mismatch between the audio and the reference is grounds for ignoring the latter. Also, no "ghost" subtitles during instrumental or silent sections. If the reference is incomplete, I'll transcribe manually.

Furthermore, I'll strictly adhere to the timecode format (MM:SS.mmm). I will prevent cascading delays by truncating subtitles rather than extending them. Each line will be capped at 50 characters, ensuring logical grouping and context-aware translation. No closed-captioning tags (CC tags) are to be used, ensuring clean output.

**Now, the breakdown of the video segment is crucial**:

*   **Initial Analysis:** The video comprises two segments: "Sweets Parade" and "Youkoso Japari Park e." I must first tackle each separately.
*   **"Sweets Parade" Examination:** I'm carefully checking the timecodes, line by line. I'm focusing on the first segment where Kana Hanazawa's original "Watanuki" (渡狸) lyric has been replaced by the "Watame" cover. I must analyze the burned-in lyrics and on-screen text for any inconsistencies or changes. I need to make sure that "渡狸" (Watanuki) is used as the written version, since "わたぬき" is only the spoken version. I am going to be extra careful here to use "渡狸" and "ワタヌキ" as appropriate, and I'll confirm that each subtitle follows proper character-limit and time-matching standards.
    *   **00:00 - 00:02:** The initial line "渡狸とキッスを" (Watanuki to kiss wo) needs careful verification, considering the visual text. I'll translate "And a kiss with Watanuki."
    *   **00:02 - 00:20:** The instrumental section is *crucially* subtitle-free.
    *   **00:21 onwards:** The on-screen text is the primary reference. I'm noting the exact spoken words and translating them accurately, and following the written display on the screen ("ミルフィーユ ワッフル ポンポン" (Mille-feuille, waffle, bonbon), "ムース 渡狸パイ" (Mousse, Watanuki pie), etc.). The subtitle will strictly follow what is shown on screen.
    *   **Spelling Verification:** I confirmed the spelling of "渡狸" or "Watanuki" is correct on the screen.
    *   **01:03-01:46** Careful analysis is required with the "A-I-U-E-O" wordplay. On screen, the text displays "愛、上、お菓子、下" (Love, above, sweets, below), reflecting the visual pun on the vocals.
*   **Transition and Title Card:** A crucial visual element is the title "ようこそジャパリパークへ" (Welcome to Japari Park). If there's no spoken audio, I'll subtitle this visual text. I'll make sure it's accurate and has correct timings. If the audio overlaps with this, I will adjust timings accordingly.
*   **"Youkoso Japari Park e" Examination:** I'll carefully check each timecode, making sure to avoid truncation. From 01:57 to the end, I'll provide translation synchronized to each lyric that follows the on-screen text.
    *   I'm translating based on audio. Also checking for exact on-screen words when present. For example, "はじまりまして" (Hajimarimashite).
